# @zakkster/lite-gc-profiler v1.2.0

Zero-dependency GC and heap profiler that makes the zero-GC claim
falsifiable. Precise perf_hooks GC entries in node, heap-drop heuristic
in Chrome, accurate opt-in measureUserAgentSpecificMemory (`uasm`)
also in Chrome under cross-origin isolation, frame-anomaly fallback
elsewhere. Three-state gate verdicts (pass/fail/inconclusive), phases,
nesting regions, differential and rep-aware gating, baselines with
fingerprinting, CLI, node:test helper, explain mode via node:inspector
heap sampling. Single-file browser surface; node-only subpaths for
register/test-helpers/explain and the lite-gc-gate CLI.

## Entry points

- `@zakkster/lite-gc-profiler` (browser-safe, main): `GcProfiler`,
  `checkNoGc`, `assertNoGc`, `compareGc`, `assertCompare`,
  `aggregateGc`, `gateReps`, `assertReps`, `captureFingerprint`,
  `createBaseline`, `checkAgainstBaseline`, `assertAgainstBaseline`,
  `formatConsole`, `formatJson`, `formatMarkdown`,
  `formatGithubAnnotations`, `GcBudgetError`, `GcInconclusiveError`,
  `GC_DEFAULT_RULES`, `GC_DEFAULT_DIFFERENTIAL_RULES`,
  `REP_POLICY_DEFAULTS`, `VERDICT_MATRIX`, `VERSION`,
  `GC_MINOR`/`GC_MAJOR`/`GC_INCREMENTAL`/`GC_WEAKCB`.
- `@zakkster/lite-gc-profiler/register` (node): auto-attach preload.
- `@zakkster/lite-gc-profiler/test-helpers` (node): `withGcGate`,
  `measureGc`.
- `@zakkster/lite-gc-profiler/explain` (node): `startExplainSampling`,
  `formatExplainConsole`.
- `bin/lite-gc-gate` (CLI).

## Key semantics

- Three-state verdict never silently green on unverifiable rules.
- Phases (linear, no nesting), regions (nested, LIFO enter/exit).
- Attribution is firing-site, not allocator. Explain mode answers the
  allocator question separately via node:inspector heap sampling.
- Rep policy defaults: majors/minors -> all-clean, pauses/rates ->
  best-clean. Override per rule.
- Baseline regression semantic: current.median > baseline.max.
- Fingerprint mismatch -> inconclusive (D6).
- CLI exit codes: 0 pass, 1 fail, 2 inconclusive, 3 infrastructure error.

## Zero-alloc guarantees

Hot-path methods (`record`, `sampleHeap`, `markFrame`, `phase`, `enter`,
`exit`) allocate nothing after the intern tables settle. Observer
callback increments one integer per batch beyond delivering entries.
Self-noise on old hardware: 0 majors, 2-4 minors, ~0.5 ms longest pause,
250-770 KB heap growth per 500ms noop window (mostly V8, not the
profiler).
